overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and well-engaged presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, primarily focusing on academic research, computational social science, and disinformation studies. With a total of 3,580 followers across these platforms and 7 recent posts, the profile demonstrates a solid foundation for disseminating research, sharing professional opportunities, and engaging with the academic community. Overall engagement is robust, with certain content types and platforms showing superior performance.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, grant awards, conference presentations, and updates on research projects like CooRnet.
Discussions and news related to algorithms, data science, computational methods for social media analysis, and research methodologies in social science.
Content focusing on detecting coordinated link sharing, information operations, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and broader disinformation studies.
Posts about hiring postdoctoral researchers and opportunities for PhD students in computational social science.

recommendations: Amplify all major research announcements (e.g., new papers, project releases) across Bluesky, given its demonstrated high engagement rate for such content. Consider tailoring content slightly for each platform's audience.

Continue to leverage LinkedIn for high-impact professional announcements, particularly grants and hiring opportunities, as it consistently attracts significant interaction due to its large professional network.
Explore more interactive content strategies on Mastodon, such as direct questions, community polls, or requests for feedback on research ideas, to further boost its already respectable engagement.

While textual updates are performing well, consider incorporating visual elements (e.g., paper cover, team photo, simple infographic) with key announcements to potentially increase reach and engagement across all platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky currently exhibits the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) per follower among all platforms. Content announcing new research papers and opportunities for PhD students performs exceptionally well, indicating a highly engaged and receptive academic audience.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon shows a solid average engagement rate (4.55%) and serves as a good platform for technical updates like new algorithm releases (CooRnet) and detailed discussions on methodological approaches to disinformation. Its community appears to value in-depth academic content.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn, with the largest follower base (1,850), generates a strong average engagement rate (4.86%). It is particularly effective for high-stakes professional announcements such as securing new grants and advertising job openings for researchers, indicating its strength for career development and institutional visibility.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
